description: This figure presents two scanning electron microscopy (SEM) secondary electron images (20 kV, ×10,000 magnification, 1 μm scale bar) illustrating discontinuous precipitation of γ + κ-carbide lamellae in Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels tempered at 600°C. Subfigure (a) shows the 9Al-10Mn-0.4C alloy, where discontinuous precipitation initiates from grain boundaries and propagates into the austenite matrix as fine lamellar colonies, leaving band-shaped untransformed γ regions. Subfigure (b) depicts the 9Al-10Mn-1C alloy, revealing nearly complete transformation of austenite into fine γ + κ lamellae with short inter-lamellar spacing, forming fan-shaped cellular colonies that dominate the microstructure. The comparison demonstrates that increasing carbon content from 0.4 wt% to 1 wt% significantly enhances the extent of discontinuous precipitation, producing finer, more densely packed carbide lamellae. According to the citation context, the 9Al-10Mn-1C alloy exhibits substantial hardening (439 HV) associated with these fine lamellae, whereas the lower-carbon variant shows only partial transformation. Additionally, the non-discontinuously precipitated austenite in 9Al-10Mn-1C may contain nano-sized κ-carbide precipitation as suggested by EDS analysis. These micrographs support the conclusion that carbon content critically controls the kinetics, morphology, and mechanical property evolution during discontinuous precipitation in these lightweight steels.
